claim: Bronchial responsiveness is higher in the summer than in the winter.

result.claim: Bronchial responsiveness is higher in the summer than in the winter.
result.reasoning: A cross-sectional study (Paper ID: 2891825) investigating bronchial hyperresponsiveness in Swedish cross-country skiers and non-skiers found "no difference in bronchial responsiveness within either group between winter and summer." This directly contradicts the claim that bronchial responsiveness is higher in the summer than in the winter. However, the study's credibility is limited as it has 0 citations and an unknown publication year, and focuses on a specific population. Therefore, while the evidence refutes the claim, the confidence is low due to the single source and its limitations.

evidence.text: A treatment response occurred in 21.9% of the PL group.
evidence.source: Efficacy and tolerability of amitriptylinoxide in the treatment of chronic tension-type headache: a multi-centre controlled study.

evidence.text: Bronchial hyperresponsiveness is much more common in cross country skiers than in the general population.
evidence.source: High prevalence of asthma in cross country skiers.

evidence.text: High relative humidity was between 40% to 65%.
evidence.source: Transfer Efficiency of Bacteria and Viruses from Porous and Nonporous Fomites to Fingers under Different Relative Humidity Conditions

evidence.text: Mitochondrial morphology is highly sensitive to changing environmental conditions.
evidence.source: Mitochondrial Fission: Regulation and ER Connection

evidence.text: The optimal timing during a given calendar year is specified for potential future transmission.
evidence.source: A Beacon in the Galaxy: Updated Arecibo Message for Potential FAST and SETI Projects

evidence.text: Transfer efficiencies were greater under high relative humidity for both porous and nonporous surfaces.
evidence.source: Transfer Efficiency of Bacteria and Viruses from Porous and Nonporous Fomites to Fingers under Different Relative Humidity Conditions

evidence.text: Nonporous surfaces had a greater transfer efficiency (up to 79.5%) than porous surfaces (<13.4%) under high relative humidity.
evidence.source: Transfer Efficiency of Bacteria and Viruses from Porous and Nonporous Fomites to Fingers under Different Relative Humidity Conditions

evidence.text: Nonporous surfaces had a greater transfer efficiency (up to 57%) than porous surfaces (<6.8%) under low relative humidity.
evidence.source: Transfer Efficiency of Bacteria and Viruses from Porous and Nonporous Fomites to Fingers under Different Relative Humidity Conditions

evidence.text: The study monitored changes in symptoms and bronchial hyperresponsiveness during the year.
evidence.source: High prevalence of asthma in cross country skiers.

evidence.text: Low physical activity increased with SDI.
evidence.source: Global, regional, and national comparative risk assessment of 79 behavioural, environmental and occupational, and metabolic risks or clusters of risks, 1990–2015: a systematic analysis for the Global Burden of Disease Study 2015

evidence.text: HCN version 2 minimum temperature trends are somewhat smaller than HCN version 1.
evidence.source: The U.S. Historical Climatology Network Monthly Temperature Data, Version 2

evidence.text: Perceived age correlated significantly with physical and cognitive functioning.
evidence.source: Perceived age as clinically useful biomarker of ageing: cohort study.

evidence.text: A 6-month program of physical activity provided a modest improvement in cognition.
evidence.source: Effect of physical activity on cognitive function in older adults at risk for Alzheimer disease: a randomized trial.

evidence.text: Nitroprusside-induced relaxation was unaffected by either chronic occlusion or exercise.
evidence.source: Endothelium-mediated relaxation of porcine collateral-dependent arterioles is improved by exercise training.

evidence.text: The study assessed the effect of a lowered ambient temperature of 16°C (61°F) compared to 22°C (72°F) on energy intake (EI), energy expenditure (EE), and respiratory quotient (RQ) in men.
evidence.source: Energy metabolism in humans at a lowered ambient temperature

evidence.text: Light can efficiently phase-delay or phase-advance circadian locomotor behavior in male Drosophila even when either the M- or the E-oscillators are ablated.
evidence.source: Neural Network Interactions Modulate CRY-Dependent Photoresponses in Drosophila

evidence.text: HCN version 2 trends in maximum temperatures are similar to HCN version 1.
evidence.source: The U.S. Historical Climatology Network Monthly Temperature Data, Version 2

evidence.text: The interquartile range (IQR) of the percentage of 12-month cases that were mild was 40.2% to 53.3%.
evidence.source: Prevalence, severity, and unmet need for treatment of mental disorders in the World Health Organization World Mental Health Surveys.

evidence.text: The interior Antarctic continent had summers warm enough for tundra vegetation to grow at an unspecified point in time.
evidence.source: The million-year evolution of the glacial trimline in the southernmost Ellsworth Mountains, Antarctica

evidence.text: Within the CB, there is a sub-group of TCB, i.e. faecal and non-faecal coliforms that are resistant to high temperatures.
evidence.source: Specific features of Escherichia coli that distinguish it from coliform and thermotolerant coliform bacteria and define it as the most accurate indicator of faecal contamination in the environment
